author: @bennbenno2388
text:
Hello Robert,

I like the first SWAG device that you made the most. I studied this phenomena of nikola tesla since more than a decade now and I once found an article of Mr. Tesla himself, writing about this Antenna. It also showed a picture of who him standing next to a pole where such a "collector plate" hang from. 

He then proceeded with the help of another man (dont remember the name unfortunatly it is now 10 years past) who simply put diffrent frequencies through the copper wire into the antenna/collector.

On the photo of the article Mr. Tesla showed that he can light over 30 bulbs only by hanging this simple atenna from a height and put frequencies into it.

Well I am no physics expert, but as far as I understood Tesla (who said the mistery of the universe is in frequency vibration and enegery)
These diffrent impulse that you give into the wire create a cymatic (look it up on youtube) pattern on the plate. This pattern is diffrent for every Hz used. So if you get a good one, all these radiant particles should flow in a specific pattern and enter the wires much more easy :) 

Unfortunatly I am not able to replicate this because I don't even really know how to build even a simple electronic circuit. But I am 100% sure that this will Improve your device because 1) tesla said so and 2) I understand a lot of cymatics vibrations and harmonics, and it sounds very logical from this point of view that it works, as I explaind above.

Hope someone took the time to read this, and light up the planet ✌️

author: @skaltura
text:
Amazing. Car companies would be interested def to commercialize this. BMW has been experimenting to use the wasted exhaust heat energy to generate electricity, but because the current method is basicly having a turbine there i think they concluded it was too restrictive and/or expensive (oil circulation and all that stuff, making half of a turbo is like 70% of the cost of a turbocharger minus the boost in performance)...

Now how about you just coat the exhaust header ...

Make this plate from heat resistant materials (800C or so), and put it in contained vessel with the solution which is aswell heat resistant. Flexible of course, and simply wrap it around the exhaust.

Infact, come to think about it, anywhere where there is wasted heat energy. Think anywhere with a chimney. Power stations have tons of wasted heat energy.

That is such a simple device that even if it is small amounts it should work out profitable commercially.

replies.author: @skaltura
replies.text:
Come to think about it, i wonder what happens when you power that sheet ... Probably heats up the solution.

Then, when you try to draw current, does it ever get to 0 output?
How about cooling the solution below ambient temp what happens then?

So many questions!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text:
I would say the speed of the molecules moving around is more suspect than the temp of the molecules, so a molecule substance that liquifies at a lower temp should give the same result as this at lower temps, question is how much of the salt is getting knocked into the plate by the other liquid as opposed to water molecules, and how they are I am sure knocking the salts against the plate, but point being is that the water vibrating around the way it does making it a liquid vs a gas or solid is more responsible for this, so if you could say introduce it to a low temp gaseous substance I would think it would give even better results since gas particles are moving about faster than liquid particles.

Just a thought here.

author: @robertshipp5415
text:
Hey Robert, great video, wonderful information! Thank you.
I am confused about the polarity of the battery output. If the SWAG plate is symmetrical with a  graphene ink coating and two copper conductors, what differentiates a positive from a negative. In other words, why does the current flow one way instead of the other way? Am I missing something simple here?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: It's ac current flow depends on water flow across the surface. So as you put it in the polarity is one way as the water flows that way then as you pull it out water flow is reversed so current flow is reversed

replies.author: @robertshipp5415
replies.text:
Robert Murray-Smith
Hey Robert, thanks for the kind reply -- but I am still a bit puzzled, especially about AC produced by the stationary SWAG in the hot aluminum chloride. For the regular dipping up and down SWAG, the electrons would still have to "know" whether to go to the left copper electrode or the right copper electrode. It would make sense if the copper strips were at top and bottom, but (unless there were a magnetic field hiding somewhere!) I do not see how you get alternating left and right current flow from an up and down motion of ions. Sorry if I am a bit dull on this, but many thanks for your work.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
Robert Shipp
it's not up and down mate it's adsorbed de adsorbed and double layer formation this matters as it changes the relation the charges have with the surface

replies.author: @ArkiverUnifiedEnergy
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith This new tech goes against some things we've been taught. That is an interesting point though. Adding a diode to each lead from the plates to give them a sense of direction should cause a charge to build in one direction and dump double voltage in the other direction. Plate acting as a capacitor.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good point mate
